question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2025

Code: 65/6/1 (set-1)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  3. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book at the given place before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This Question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Question paper is divided into FIVE Sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Question Number 1 to 18 are Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and Question Number {tex}19 \& 20{/tex} are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Question Number 21 to 25 are Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Question Number 26 to 31 are Short Answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Question Number 32 to 35 are Long Answer (LA) type questions, carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Question Number 36 to 38 are case study based questions, carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section - B, 3 questions in Section - C, 2 questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{D}{/tex} and {tex}{2}{/tex} questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{E}{/tex}.
  9. Use of calculator is NOT allowed.

Section - A

This section comprises multiple choice questions (MCQs) of 1 mark each.
1.

Let both {tex}A B^{\prime}{/tex} and {tex}B^{\prime} A{/tex} be defined for matrices {tex}A{/tex} and {tex}B{/tex}. If order of {tex}A{/tex} is {tex}{n} \times {m}{/tex}, then the order of B is:

a. {tex}{n} \times {n}{/tex}
b. {tex}n \times m{/tex}
c. {tex}{m} \times {m}{/tex}
d. {tex}{m} \times {n}{/tex}
1
2.

If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then A is {tex}{a} / {an}{/tex}:

a. scalar matrix
b. identity matrix
c. symmetric matrix
d. skew-symmetric matrix
1
3.

The following graph is a combination of:

a. {tex}y=\sin ^{-1} x{/tex} and {tex}y=\cos ^{-1} x{/tex}
b. {tex}y=\cos ^{-1} x{/tex} and {tex}y=\cos x{/tex}
c. {tex}y=\sin ^{-1} x{/tex} and {tex}y=\sin x{/tex}
d. {tex}y=\cos ^{-1} x{/tex} and {tex}y=\sin x{/tex}
1
4.

Sum of two skew-symmetric matrices of same order is always a/an:

a. skew-symmetric matrix
b. symmetric matrix
c. null matrix
d. identity matrix
1
5.

{tex}\left[\sec ^{-1}(-\sqrt{2})-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)\right]{/tex} is equal to:

a. {tex}\frac{11 \pi}{12}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{5 \pi}{12}{/tex}
c. {tex}-\frac{5 \pi}{12}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{7 \pi}{12}{/tex}
1
6.

If {tex}{f}({x})=\left\{\begin{array}{cl}\frac{\log (1+{ax})+\log (1-{bx})}{{x}}, & \text { for } {x} \neq 0 \\ {k} & , \text { for } {x}=0\end{array}\right.{/tex} is continuous at {tex}x=0{/tex}, then the value of {tex}k{/tex} is:

a. a
b. {tex}{a}+{b}{/tex}
c. {tex}{a}-{b}{/tex}
d. b
1
7.

If {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right)=a{/tex}, where '{tex}a{/tex}' is a constant, then {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
b. {tex}-\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{{a}}{{x}}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{{a}}{{y}}{/tex}
1
8.

If {tex}y=a \cos (\log x)+b \sin (\log x){/tex}, then {tex}x^{2} y_{2}+x y_{1}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\cot (\log {x}){/tex}
b. y
c. -y
d. {tex}\tan (\log {x}){/tex}
1
9.

Let {tex}f(x)=|x|, x \in R{/tex}. Then, which of the following statements is incorrect?

a. f has a minimum value at {tex}{x}=0{/tex}.
b. f has no maximum value in {tex}R{/tex}.
c. {tex}f{/tex} is continuous at {tex}x=0{/tex}.
d. f is differentiable at {tex}{x}=0{/tex}.
1
10.

Let {tex}{f}^{\prime}({x})=3\left({x}^{2}+2 {x}\right)-\frac{4}{{x}^{3}}+5, {f}(1)=0{/tex}. Then, {tex}{f}({x}){/tex} is:

a. {tex}{x}^{3}+3 {x}^{2}+\frac{2}{{x}^{2}}+5 {x}+11{/tex}
b. {tex}{x}^{3}+3 {x}^{2}+\frac{2}{{x}^{2}}+5 {x}-11{/tex}
c. {tex}{x}^{3}+3 {x}^{2}-\frac{2}{{x}^{2}}+5 {x}-11{/tex}
d. {tex}{x}^{3}-3 {x}^{2}-\frac{2}{{x}^{2}}+5 {x}-11{/tex}
1
11.

{tex}\int \frac{{x}+5}{({x}+6)^{2}} {e}^{{x}} {dx}{/tex} is equal to:

a. {tex}\log ({x}+6)+{C}{/tex}
b. {tex}{e}^{{x}}+{C}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{{e}^{{x}}}{{x}+6}+{C}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{-1}{(x+6)^{2}}+C{/tex}
1
12.

The order and degree of the following differential equation are, respectively:
{tex}-\frac{d^{4} y}{d x^{4}}+2 e^{d y / d x}+y^{2}=0{/tex}

a. {tex}-4,1{/tex}
b. 4, not defined
c. 1,1
d. 4,1
1
13.

The solution for the differential equation {tex}\log \left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=3 x+4 y{/tex} is:

a. {tex}3 {e}^{4 {y}}+4 {e}^{-3 {x}}+{C}=0{/tex}
b. {tex}{e}^{3 {x}+4 {y}}+{C}=0{/tex}
c. {tex}3 {e}^{-3 {y}}+4 {e}^{4 {x}}+12 {C}=0{/tex}
d. {tex}3 {e}^{-4 {y}}+4 {e}^{3 {x}}+12 {C}=0{/tex}
1
14.

For a Linear Programming Problem (LPP), the given objective function is {tex}{Z}={x}+2 {y}{/tex}. The feasible region PQRS determined by the set of constraints is shown as a shaded region in the graph.

{tex}{P} \equiv\left(\frac{3}{13}, \frac{24}{13}\right), {Q} \equiv\left(\frac{3}{2}, \frac{15}{4}\right), {R} \equiv\left(\frac{7}{2}, \frac{3}{4}\right), {S} \equiv\left(\frac{18}{7}, \frac{2}{7}\right){/tex}
Which of the following statements is correct?

a. {tex}{Z}{/tex} is minimum at {tex}{S}\left(\frac{18}{7}, \frac{2}{7}\right){/tex}
b. {tex}{Z}{/tex} is maximum at {tex}{R}\left(\frac{7}{2}, \frac{3}{4}\right){/tex}
c. {tex}({/tex}Value of Z at P{tex}) \gt ({/tex}Value of Z at Q{tex}){/tex}
d. (Value of Z at Q{tex}) \lt ({/tex}Value of Z at R)
1
15.

In a Linear Programming Problem (LPP), the objective function {tex}{Z}=2 {x}+5 {y}{/tex} is to be maximised under the following constraints:
{tex}x+y \leq 4,3 x+3 y \geq 18, x, y \geq 0{/tex}
Study the graph and select the correct option.

The solution of the given LPP:

a. lies in the shaded unbounded region.
b. lies in {tex}\triangle {AOB}{/tex}.
c. does not exist.
d. lies in the combined region of {tex}\triangle {AOB}{/tex} and unbounded shaded region.
1
16.

Let {tex}|\vec{a}|=5{/tex} and {tex}-2 \leq \lambda \leq 1{/tex}. Then, the range of {tex}|\lambda \vec{a}|{/tex} is:

a. {tex}[5,10]{/tex}
b. {tex}[-2,5]{/tex}
c. {tex}[-2,1]{/tex}
d. {tex}[-10,5]{/tex}
1
17.

The area of the region bounded by the curve {tex}y^{2}=x{/tex} between {tex}x=0{/tex} and {tex}{x}=1{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex} sq units
b. {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} sq units
c. 3 sq units
d. {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex} sq units
1
18.

A box has 4 green, 8 blue and 3 red pens. A student picks up a pen at random, checks its colour and replaces it in the box. He repeats this process 3 times. The probability that at least one pen picked was red is:

a. {tex}\frac{124}{125}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{1}{125}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{61}{125}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{64}{125}{/tex}
1

Questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion and Reason based questions. Two statements are given, one labelled Assertion (A) and the other labelled Reason (R). Select the correct answer from the codes (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below.

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
19.

Assertion (A): If {tex}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|^{2}+|\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}|^{2}=256{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{b}|=8{/tex}, then {tex}|\vec{a}|=2 .{/tex}
Reason {tex}(R): \sin ^{2} \theta+\cos ^{2} \theta=1{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \sin \theta \text { and } \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \theta{/tex}

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
20.

Assertion (A): Let {tex}{f}({x})={e}^{{x}}{/tex} and {tex}{g}({x})=\log {x}{/tex}. Then {tex}({f}+{g}) {x}={e}^{{x}}+\log {x}{/tex} where domain of ({tex}f+g{/tex}) is {tex}R{/tex}.
Reason (R): {tex} \operatorname{Dom}({f}+{g})=\operatorname{Dom}({f}) \cap \operatorname{Dom}({g}){/tex}.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

Section - B

This section comprises 5 Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
21.

Find the domain of {tex}f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(-x^{2}\right){/tex}.

 2
22.(i)

Differentiate {tex}\sqrt{{e}^{\sqrt{2 {x}}}}{/tex} with respect to {tex}{e}^{\sqrt{2 {x}}}{/tex} for {tex}{x} \gt 0{/tex}.

 2

OR
(ii)

If {tex}(x)^{y}=(y)^{x}{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.

 2
23.

Determine the values of x for which {tex}{f}({x})=\frac{{x}-4}{{x}+1}, {x} \neq-1{/tex} is an increasing or a decreasing function.

 2
24.(i)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are position vectors of point {tex}A{/tex} and point {tex}B{/tex} respectively, find the position vector of point C on BA produced such that {tex}{BC}=3 {BA}{/tex}.

 2

OR
(ii)

Vector {tex}\vec{r}{/tex} is inclined at equal angles to the three axes {tex}x, y{/tex} and z. If magnitude of {tex}\vec{r}{/tex} is {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} units, then find {tex}\vec{r}{/tex}.

 2
25.

Determine if the lines {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k})+\lambda(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{r}=(4 \hat{i}-\hat{k})+\mu(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} intersect with each other.

 2

Section - C

This section comprises 6 Short Answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.

Let {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{r}1 \\ 4 \\ -2\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{C}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & 4 & 2 \\ 12 & 16 & 8 \\ -6 & -8 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex} be two matrices. Then, find the matrix B if {tex}{AB}={C}{/tex}.

 3
27.(i)

Differentiate {tex}{y}=\sin ^{-1}\left(3 {x}-4 {x}^{3}\right){/tex} w.r.t. x , if {tex}{x} \in\left[-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{1}{2}\right]{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

Differentiate {tex}{y}=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-{x}^{2}}{1+{x}^{2}}\right){/tex} with respect to x, when {tex}{x} \in(0,1){/tex}.

 3
28.(i)

A student wants to pair up natural numbers in such a way that they satisfy the equation {tex}2 {x}+{y}=41, {x}, {y} \in {N}{/tex}. Find the domain and range of the relation. Check if the relation thus formed is reflexive, symmetric and transitive. Hence, state whether it is an equivalence relation or not.

 3

OR
(ii)

Show that the function {tex}{f}: {N} \rightarrow {N}{/tex}, where N is a set of natural numbers, given by {tex}f(n)=\left\{\begin{array}{l}n-1, \text { if } n \text { is even } \\ n+1, \text { if } n \text { is odd }\end{array}\right.{/tex} is a bijection.

 3
29.

Consider the Linear Programming Problem, where the objective function {tex}{Z}=({x}+4 {y}){/tex} needs to be minimized subject to constraints
{tex}2 x+y \geq 1000{/tex}
{tex}x+2 y \geq 800{/tex}
{tex}x, y \geq 0{/tex}
Draw a neat graph of the feasible region and find the minimum value of Z.

 3
30.(i)

Find the distance of a point (2, 4, –1) from the line {tex}\frac{{x + 5}}{1} = \frac{{y + 3}}{4} = \frac{{z - 6}}{{ - 9}}{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

Let the position vectors of the points {tex}A, B{/tex} and {tex}C{/tex} be {tex}3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{i}+5 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} respectively. Find the vector and cartesian equations of the line passing through A and parallel to line BC.

 3
31.

A person is Head of two independent selection committees I and II. If the probability of making a wrong selection in committee I is 0.03 and that in committee II is {tex}0.01{/tex}, then find the probability that the person makes the correct decision of selection:

  1. in both committees
  2. in only one committee
 3

Section - D

This section comprises 4 Long Answer (LA) type questions of 5 marks each.
32.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{x^{2}+1}{(x-1)^{2}(x+3)} d x{/tex}

 5

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex}

 5
33.

Draw a rough sketch for the curve {tex}y=2+|x+1|{/tex}. Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the curve {tex}{y}=2+|{x}+1|, {x}=-4, {x}=3{/tex} and {tex}{y}=0{/tex}.

 5
34.(i)

Solve the differential equation: {tex}x^{2} y d x-\left(x^{3}+y^{3}\right) d y=0{/tex}

 5

OR
(ii)

Solve the differential equation: (1 + x2) {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + 2xy - 4x2 = 0, subject to the initial condition y(0) = 0.

 5
35.

Let the polished side of the mirror be along the line {tex}\frac{x}{1}=\frac{1-y}{-2}=\frac{2 z-4}{6}{/tex}.
A point {tex}{P}(1,6,3){/tex}, some distance away from the mirror, has its image formed behind the mirror. Find the coordinates of the image point and the distance between the point P and its image.

 5

Section - E

This section comprises 3 case study-based questions of 4 marks each

Case Study - 1
36.

Three students, Neha, Rani and Sam go to a market to purchase stationery items. Neha buys 4 pens, 3 notepads and 2 erasers and pays ₹ 60. Rani buys 2 pens, 4 notepads and 6 erasers for ₹ 90. Sam pays ₹ 70 for 6 pens, 2 notepads and 3 erasers.
Based upon the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Form the equations required to solve the problem of finding the price of each item, and express it in the matrix form {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex}. (1)
  2. Find {tex}|{A}|{/tex} and confirm if it is possible to find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}. (1)
    1. Find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}, if possible, and write the formula to find X. (2)

      OR

    2. Find {tex}{A}^{2}-8 {I}{/tex}, where I is an identity matrix. (2)
 4

Case Study - 2
37.


A ladder of fixed length '{tex}h{/tex}' is to be placed along the wall such that it is free to move along the height of the wall.
Based upon the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Express the distance (y) between the wall and foot of the ladder in terms of '{tex}h{/tex}' and height ( {tex}x{/tex} ) on the wall at a certain instant. Also, write an expression in terms of {tex}h{/tex} and {tex}x{/tex} for the area (A) of the right triangle, as seen from the side by an observer. (1)
  2. Find the derivative of the area (A) with respect to the height on the wall (x), and find its critical point. (1)
    1. Show that the area (A) of the right triangle is maximum at the critical point. (2)

      OR

    2. If the foot of the ladder whose length is 5 m , is being pulled towards the wall such that the rate of decrease of distance (y) is {tex}2 {~m} / {s}{/tex}, then at what rate is the height on the wall (x) increasing, when the foot of the ladder is 3 m away from the wall? (2)
 4

Case Study - 3
38.

A shop selling electronic items sells smartphones of only three reputed companies {tex}{A}, {B}{/tex} and C because chances of their manufacturing a defective smartphone are only {tex}5 \%, 4 \%{/tex} and {tex}2 \%{/tex} respectively. In his inventory he has 25% smartphones from company A, 35% smartphones from company B and {tex}40 \%{/tex} smartphones from company C.
A person buys a smartphone from this shop.

  1. Find the probability that it was defective. (2)
  2. What is the probability that this defective smartphone was manufactured by company B? (2)
 4

Evaluate: {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\text { Let } I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)+\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)} d x{/tex}
By using property of definite integrals,{tex} \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x{/tex}, we get
{tex} I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{x+\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\frac{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}+\frac{1-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}+1-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}+2 \tan \frac{x}{2}+1} d x{/tex}
{tex}\text { Let } \quad \tan \frac{x}{2}=t . \text { Then, } d\left(\tan \frac{x}{2}\right)={/tex}{tex}d t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} d x=d t \Rightarrow d x=\frac{2 d t}{\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{/tex}
Also, {tex}x=0 \Rightarrow t=\tan 0=0 \text { and } x=\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow t=\tan \frac{\pi}{4}=1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 d t}{-t^{2}+2 t+1}={/tex}{tex}\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{-\left(t^{2}-2 t-1\right)}=\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{-\left[(t-1)^{2}-2\right]}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{(\sqrt{2})^{2}-(t-1)^{2}}{/tex}{tex}=\pi \times\left.\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\log | \frac{\sqrt{2}+t-1}{\sqrt{2}-t+1}\right]\right|_{0} ^{1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\log 1-\log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}\right)\right]{/tex}{tex}=-\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}}{(\sqrt{2}-1)(\sqrt{2}+1)}\right\}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}=\frac{\pi}{\sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1){/tex}

34.(i)

Solve the differential equation: {tex}x^{2} y d x-\left(x^{3}+y^{3}\right) d y=0{/tex}

Solution:

Given differential equation can be written as {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y x^{2}}{x^{3}+y^{3}}{/tex}
Put {tex}y=v x{/tex}, so {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{v x^{3}}{x^{3}+v^{3} x^{3}}=\frac{v}{1+v^{3}}{/tex}
{tex}x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-v^{4}}{1+v^{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\frac{1}{v^{4}}+\frac{1}{v}\right) d v=\frac{-d x}{x}{/tex}
Integrating we get
{tex}\frac{-1}{3 v^{3}}+\log |v|=-\log |x|+C{/tex}
{tex}\frac{-{x}^{3}}{3 {y}^{3}}+\log |y|={C}{/tex}

OR
(ii)

Solve the differential equation: (1 + x2) {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + 2xy - 4x2 = 0, subject to the initial condition y(0) = 0.

Solution:

Given: {tex}\left(1+x^{2}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+2 x y-4 x^{2}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{2 x y}{1+x^{2}}-\frac{4 x^{2}}{1+x^{2}}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{2 x y}{1+x^{2}}=\frac{4 x^{2}}{1+x^{2}}{/tex}
Here, {tex}P=\frac{2 x }{1+x^{2}}{/tex} and {tex}Q=\frac{4 x^{2}}{1+x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{I.F}=e^{\int P d x}=e^{\int \frac{2 x}{1+x^{2}} d x}{/tex}
{tex}\text { I.F. }=e^{\log \left|1+x^{2}\right|}{/tex}
I.F. = 1 + x2 {tex}\left[\because e^{\log x}=x\right]{/tex}
Solution of given differential equation is
{tex}y(\mathrm{I.F})=\int(\mathrm{I} \cdot \mathrm{F} \times \mathrm{Q}) d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad y\left(1+x^{2}\right)=\int\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(\frac{4 x^{2}}{1+x^{2}}\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad y\left(1+x^{2}\right)=\frac{4 x^{3}}{3}+C{/tex} ...(i)
Now, putting x = 0, y = 0 in (i), we get
0 = 0 + C
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C = 0
Substitute the value of C in (i), we get
{tex}y\left(1+x^{2}\right)=\frac{4 x^{3}}{3}{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{4 x^{3}}{3\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{/tex}
